[Tool_Request] BackendDeveloper: write_text_file({"filename":"app.py","content":"from flask import Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash\nimport os\nfrom werkzeug.utils import secure_filename\n\napp = Flask(__name__)\napp.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'dev-secret-key'\n\nDATA_PATH = 'data'\n\n\n# Helper Functions for File Reads\n\ndef read_jobs():\n    jobs_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'jobs.txt')\n    jobs = []\n    if os.path.exists(jobs_file):\n        with open(jobs_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line:\n                    continue\n                # Fields: job_id|title|category_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date\n                parts = line.split('|')\n                if len(parts) != 9:\n                    continue\n                job = {\n                    'job_id': int(parts[0]),\n                    'title': parts[1],\n                    'category_id': int(parts[2]),\n                    'location': parts[3],\n                    'salary_min': int(parts[4]),\n                    'salary_max': int(parts[5]),\n                    'category': parts[6],\n                    'description': parts[7],\n                    'posted_date': parts[8]\n                }\n                jobs.append(job)\n    return jobs\n\n\ndef read_categories():\n    categories_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'job_categories.txt')\n    categories = {}\n    if os.path.exists(categories_file):\n        with open(categories_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line:\n                    continue\n                parts = line.split('|')\n                if len(parts) != 2:\n                    continue\n                try:\n                    cid = int(parts[0])\n                    cname = parts[1]\n                    categories[cid] = cname\n                except ValueError:\n                    continue\n    return categories\n\n\ndef read_companies():\n    companies_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'companies.txt')\n    companies = []\n    if os.path.exists(companies_file):\n        with open(companies_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line:\n                    continue\n                # Expected fields: company_id|company_name|location|industry|employee_count|description\n                parts = line.split('|')\n                if len(parts) != 6:\n                    continue\n                try:\n                    company = {\n                        'company_id': int(parts[0]),\n                        'company_name': parts[1],\n                        'location': parts[2],\n                        'industry': parts[3],\n                        'employee_count': int(parts[4]),\n                        'description': parts[5]\n                    }\n                    companies.append(company)\n                except ValueError:\n                    continue\n    return companies\n\n\ndef read_applications():\n    applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')\n    applications = []\n    if os.path.exists(applications_file):\n        with open(applications_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line:\n                    continue\n                # Fields: application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id\n                parts = line.split('|')\n                if len(parts) != 7:\n                    continue\n                try:\n                    app = {\n                        'application_id': int(parts[0]),\n                        'job_id': int(parts[1]),\n                        'applicant_name': parts[2],\n                        'applicant_email': parts[3],\n                        'status': parts[4],\n                        'applied_date': parts[5],\n                        'resume_id': int(parts[6])\n                    }\n                    applications.append(app)\n                except ValueError:\n                    continue\n    return applications\n\n\ndef read_resumes():\n    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')\n    resumes = []\n    if os.path.exists(resumes_file):\n        with open(resumes_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line:\n                    continue\n                # Fields: resume_id|applicant_name|filename|upload_date|summary\n                parts = line.split('|')\n                if len(parts) != 5:\n                    continue\n                try:\n                    resume = {\n                        'resume_id': int(parts[0]),\n                        'applicant_name': parts[1],\n                        'filename': parts[2],\n                        'upload_date': parts[3],\n                        'summary': parts[4]\n                    }\n                    resumes.append(resume)\n                except ValueError:\n                    continue\n    return resumes\n\n\ndef write_applications(applications):\n    applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')\n    with open(applications_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for app in applications:\n            line = f\"{app['application_id']}|{app['job_id']}|{app['applicant_name']}|{app['applicant_email']}|{app['status']}|{app['applied_date']}|{app['resume_id']}\"\n            f.write(line + '\\n')\n\n\n# Root Redirect to /dashboard\n@app.route('/')\ndef root():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard_view'))\n\n\n# Dashboard View - GET - shows jobs with limited info\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard_view():\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    # Prepare summary info for dashboard\n    jobs_summary = []\n    for j in jobs:\n        job_summary = {\n            'job_id': j['job_id'],\n            'title': j['title'],\n            'location': j['location'],\n            'salary_min': j['salary_min'],\n            'salary_max': j['salary_max'],\n            'category': j['category']\n        }\n        jobs_summary.append(job_summary)\n    return render_template('dashboard.html', jobs=jobs_summary)\n\n\n# Jobs Listings Page GET\n@app.route('/jobs')\ndef job_listings():\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    # Extract category names\n    categories = read_categories()\n    # We can include category name through category_id lookup\n    for job in jobs:\n        job['category'] = categories.get(job['category_id'], 'Unknown')\n    return render_template('job_listings.html', jobs=jobs)\n\n\n# Job Details GET\n@app.route('/job/<int:job_id>')\ndef job_details(job_id):\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    job = next((j for j in jobs if j['job_id'] == job_id), None)\n    if not job:\n        flash('Job not found')\n        return redirect(url_for('job_listings'))\n\n    # Also get company name from companies dataset based on job's category or job_id (No exact mapping given, so for demo we'll skip)\n    companies = read_companies()\n    company_name = ''\n    for c in companies:\n        # If job_id or category_id maps to company_id we use this heuristic, else empty\n        # No mapping provided, so we leave blank\n        pass\n\n    return render_template('job_details.html', job=job, company_name=company_name)\n\n\n# Apply to Job POST\n@app.route('/apply/<int:job_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef apply_job(job_id):\n    applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')\n    applicant_email = request.form.get('applicant_email')\n    cover_letter = request.form.get('cover_letter')\n    resume_file = request.files.get('resume')\n\n    if not all([applicant_name, applicant_email, cover_letter, resume_file]):\n        flash('All fields are required including the resume file.')\n        return redirect(url_for('job_details', job_id=job_id))\n\n    # Save resume to a static folder or data folder (for the example, save to data/resumes_upload/)\n    resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')\n    os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)\n    filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)\n    save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)\n    resume_file.save(save_path)\n\n    # Read resumes and add new resume with a new resume_id\n    resumes = read_resumes()\n    max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)\n    new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1\n\n    # Add new resume metadata\n    resumes.append({\n        'resume_id': new_resume_id,\n        'applicant_name': applicant_name,\n        'filename': filename,\n        'upload_date': '2025-01-01',  # For demo placeholder, ideally use current date\n        'summary': cover_letter[:100]  # store first 100 chars\n    })\n\n    # Save resumes metadata\n    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')\n    with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for r in resumes:\n            line = f\"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}\"\n            f.write(line + '\\n')\n\n    # Read current applications and append new application\n    applications = read_applications()\n    max_app_id = max([a['application_id'] for a in applications], default=0)\n    new_app_id = max_app_id + 1\n\n    new_application = {\n        'application_id': new_app_id,\n        'job_id': job_id,\n        'applicant_name': applicant_name,\n        'applicant_email': applicant_email,\n        'status': 'Applied',\n        'applied_date': '2025-01-01',  # Placeholder current date\n        'resume_id': new_resume_id\n    }\n    applications.append(new_application)\n    write_applications(applications)\n\n    flash('Application submitted successfully!')\n    return redirect(url_for('application_tracking'))\n\n\n# Application Tracking GET\n@app.route('/applications')\ndef application_tracking():\n    applications = read_applications()\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    companies = read_companies()\n\n    # Build application display list with company_name from jobs\n    jobs_map = {j['job_id']: j for j in jobs}\n\n    display_apps = []\n    for app in applications:\n        job = jobs_map.get(app['job_id'])\n        if job:\n            display_app = {\n                'application_id': app['application_id'],\n                'job_title': job['title'],\n                'company_name': '',  # No direct company mapping provided\n                'status': app['status'],\n                'applied_date': app['applied_date']\n            }\n            display_apps.append(display_app)\n\n    return render_template('application_tracking.html', applications=display_apps)\n\n\n# Companies Directory GET\n@app.route('/companies')\ndef companies_directory():\n    companies = read_companies()\n    return render_template('companies_directory.html', companies=companies)\n\n\n# Company Profile with Jobs GET\n@app.route('/company/<int:company_id>')\ndef company_profile(company_id):\n    companies = read_companies()\n    company = next((c for c in companies if c['company_id'] == company_id), None)\n    if not company:\n        flash('Company not found')\n        return redirect(url_for('companies_directory'))\n\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    company_jobs = [job for job in jobs if False]  # No clear mapping of jobs to company, so empty\n\n    return render_template('company_profile.html', company=company, jobs=company_jobs)\n\n\n# Resume Management GET\n@app.route('/resumes', methods=['GET', 'POST'])\ndef resume_management():\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        # Upload resume logic\n        resume_file = request.files.get('resume_file')\n        applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')\n        if not resume_file or not applicant_name:\n            flash('Applicant name and resume file required')\n            return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))\n\n        filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)\n        resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')\n        os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)\n        save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)\n        resume_file.save(save_path)\n\n        resumes = read_resumes()\n        max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)\n        new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1\n\n        # For demo, upload_date static\n        new_resume = {\n            'resume_id': new_resume_id,\n            'applicant_name': applicant_name,\n            'filename': filename,\n            'upload_date': '2025-01-01',\n            'summary': ''\n        }\n        resumes.append(new_resume)\n\n        resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')\n        with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n            for r in resumes:\n                line = f\"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}\"\n                f.write(line + '\\n')\n\n        flash('Resume uploaded successfully')\n        return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))\n\n    # GET\n    resumes = read_resumes()\n    return render_template('resume_management.html', resumes=resumes)\n\n\n# Delete Resume POST\n@app.route('/resumes/delete/<int:resume_id>', methods=['POST'])\ndef delete_resume(resume_id):\n    resumes = read_resumes()\n    resumes = [r for r in resumes if r['resume_id'] != resume_id]\n\n    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')\n    with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for r in resumes:\n            line = f\"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}\"\n            f.write(line + '\\n')\n\n    flash('Resume deleted successfully')\n    return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))\n\n\n# Search GET\n@app.route('/search')\ndef search_query():\n    query = request.args.get('query', '').strip().lower()\n    jobs = read_jobs()\n    companies = read_companies()\n\n    job_results = []\n    company_results = []\n    if query:\n        for job in jobs:\n            if query in job['title'].lower() or query in job['category'].lower():\n                job_results.append(job)\n\n        for c in companies:\n            if query in c['company_name'].lower() or query in c['industry'].lower():\n                company_results.append(c)\n\n    return render_template('search_results.html', search_query=query, job_results=job_results, company_results=company_results)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    app.run(debug=True)\n"})

jobs : list of dict with job_id (int), title (str), status (str)           | None                                                                                                                           | | /resumes                      | resume_management      | GET, POST    | resume_management.html  | resumes : list of dict with resume_id (int), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), filename (str), upload_date (str), summary (str) | (POST) resume_file (file upload), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), summary (str)                                   | | /resumes/delete/<int:resume_id>| delete_resume          | POST         | None (Redirect after deletion) | None                                                                                                                             | None (Resume ID from URL)                                                                                                     | | /search                       | search_results         | GET          | search_results.html     | search_query : str, job_results : list of dict(job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)), company_results : list of dict(company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str)) | None                                                                                                                           |  ---  ## 2. HTML Template Specifications  ### 1. Dashboard Page - Filename: templates/dashboard.html - Page Title: Job Board Dashboard - Elements:   - ID: dashboard-page (Div) - Container for the dashboard page   - ID: featured-jobs (Div) - Display of featured job recommendations   - ID: browse-jobs-button (Button) - Navigates to Job Listings Page   - ID: my-applications-button (Button) - Navigates to Application Tracking Page   - ID: companies-button (Button) - Navigates to Companies Directory Page - Context Variables:   - featured_jobs: List of dicts with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - company_name (str)     - location (str)     - salary_min (int)     - salary_max (int)     - category (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - browse-jobs-button: url_for('job_listings')   - my-applications-button: url_for('application_tracking')   - companies-button: url_for('companies_directory')  ### 2. Job Listings Page - Filename: templates/job_listings.html - Page Title: Job Listings - Elements:   - ID: listings-page (Div) - Container for the listings page   - ID: search-input (Input) - Search jobs by title, company, or location   - ID: category-filter (Dropdown) - Filter jobs by category   - ID: location-filter (Dropdown) - Filter jobs by location   - ID: jobs-grid (Div) - Grid displaying job cards   - ID: view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button) - Button to view job details for each job - Context Variables:   - jobs: List of dicts with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - company_name (str)     - location (str)     - salary_min (int)     - salary_max (int)     - category (str)   - categories: List of dicts with keys:     - category_id (int)     - category_name (str)   - locations: List of str - Navigation Mappings:   - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)  ### 3. Job Details Page - Filename: templates/job_details.html - Page Title: Job Details - Elements:   - ID: job-details-page (Div) - Container for the job details page   - ID: job-title (H1) - Displays job title   - ID: company-name (Div) - Displays company name   - ID: job-description (Div) - Displays full job description and requirements   - ID: salary-range (Div) - Displays salary range   - ID: apply-now-button (Button) - Button to apply for the job - Context Variables:   - job: dict with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - company_name (str)     - location (str)     - salary_min (int)     - salary_max (int)     - category (str)     - description (str)   - company: dict with keys:     - company_id (int)     - company_name (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - apply-now-button: url_for('application_form', job_id=job.job_id)  ### 4. Application Form Page - Filename: templates/application_form.html - Page Title: Submit Application - Elements:   - ID: application-form-page (Div) - Container for the application form   - ID: applicant-name (Input) - Input for applicant name   - ID: applicant-email (Input) - Input for applicant email   - ID: resume-upload (File Input) - Upload resume file   - ID: cover-letter (Textarea) - Input for cover letter   - ID: submit-application-button (Button) - Submit application - Context Variables:   - job: dict with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - company_name (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - submit-application-button: form POST to url_for('application_form', job_id=job.job_id)  ### 5. Application Tracking Page - Filename: templates/application_tracking.html - Page Title: My Applications - Elements:   - ID: tracking-page (Div) - Container for tracking page   - ID: applications-table (Table) - Displays applications with columns: job title, company, status, date applied   - ID: status-filter (Dropdown) - Filter applications by status   - ID: view-application-button-{{ app.application_id }} (Button) - View application details per application   - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate to dashboard - Context Variables:   - applications: List of dicts with keys:     - application_id (int)     - job_title (str)     - company_name (str)     - status (str)     - applied_date (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - view-application-button-{{ app.application_id }}: (Optional, not specified in user task)   - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')  ### 6. Companies Directory Page - Filename: templates/companies_directory.html - Page Title: Company Directory - Elements:   - ID: companies-page (Div) - Container for companies page   - ID: companies-list (Div) - List of company cards   - ID: search-company-input (Input) - Search companies by name or industry   - ID: view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }} (Button) - View company profile   - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate to dashboard - Context Variables:   - companies: List of dicts with keys:     - company_id (int)     - company_name (str)     - industry (str)     - employee_count (int) - Navigation Mappings:   - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)   - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')  ### 7. Company Profile Page - Filename: templates/company_profile.html - Page Title: Company Profile - Elements:   - ID: company-profile-page (Div) - Container for company profile   - ID: company-info (Div) - Displays company name, industry, location, description   - ID: company-jobs (Div) - Displays all open jobs for company   - ID: jobs-list (Div) - List of jobs with titles and status   - ID: view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button) - View job details from company profile   - ID: back-to-companies (Button) - Navigate to companies directory - Context Variables:   - company: dict with keys:     - company_id (int)     - company_name (str)     - industry (str)     - location (str)     - description (str)   - jobs: List of dicts with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - status (str)  # status if available, else could be inferred as 'Open' - Navigation Mappings:   - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)   - back-to-companies: url_for('companies_directory')  ### 8. Resume Management Page - Filename: templates/resume_management.html - Page Title: My Resumes - Elements:   - ID: resume-page (Div) - Container for resume page   - ID: resumes-list (Div) - List of uploaded resumes   - ID: upload-resume-button (Button) - Upload a new resume   - ID: resume-file-input (File Input) - Hidden file input for upload   - ID: delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button) - Delete a resume   - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate back to dashboard - Context Variables:   - resumes: List of dicts with keys:     - resume_id (int)     - applicant_name (str)     - applicant_email (str)     - filename (str)     - upload_date (str)     - summary (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - upload-resume-button: triggers click on hidden resume-file-input   - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: POST to url_for('delete_resume', resume_id=resume.resume_id)   - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')  ### 9. Search Results Page - Filename: templates/search_results.html - Page Title: Search Results - Elements:   - ID: search-results-page (Div) - Container for search results   - ID: search-query-display (Div) - Displays the search query string   - ID: results-tabs (Div) - Tabs to switch between job and company results   - ID: job-results (Div) - Displays search results for jobs   - ID: company-results (Div) - Displays search results for companies   - ID: no-results-message (Div) - Displays when no search results - Context Variables:   - search_query: str   - job_results: List of dicts with keys:     - job_id (int)     - title (str)     - company_name (str)     - location (str)     - salary_min (int)     - salary_max (int)   - company_results: List of dicts with keys:     - company_id (int)     - company_name (str)     - industry (str) - Navigation Mappings:   - job-results's view buttons (if any): url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)   - company-results's view buttons (if any): url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)  ---  ## 3. Data File Schemas  ### 1. jobs.txt - File Path: data/jobs.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - job_id (int)   - title (str)   - company_id (int)   - location (str)   - salary_min (int)   - salary_max (int)   - category (str)   - description (str)   - posted_date (YYYY-MM-DD string) - Data Description: Contains all job postings with relevant details including company association, location, salary range, category, and the posting date. - Example Rows:   ```   1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15   2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16   3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14   ```  ### 2. companies.txt - File Path: data/companies.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - company_id (int)   - company_name (str)   - industry (str)   - location (str)   - employee_count (int)   - description (str) - Data Description: Contains records of companies with identifying information and descriptions. - Example Rows:   ```   1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider   2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company   3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization   ```  ### 3. categories.txt - File Path: data/categories.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - category_id (int)   - category_name (str)   - description (str) - Data Description: Defines job categories with description - Example Rows:   ```   1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions   2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions   3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions   ```  ### 4. applications.txt - File Path: data/applications.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - application_id (int)   - job_id (int)   - applicant_name (str)   - applicant_email (str)   - status (str) # e.g., Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected   - applied_date (YYYY-MM-DD string)   - resume_id (int) - Data Description: Contains user's job applications with status and related resume - Example Rows:   ```   1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1   2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2   3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3   ```  ### 5. resumes.txt - File Path: data/resumes.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - resume_id (int)   - applicant_name (str)   - applicant_email (str)   - filename (str) # uploaded file name   - upload_date (YYYY-MM-DD string)   - summary (str) # short description or summary of resume - Data Description: User uploaded resumes with metadata - Example Rows:   ```   1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience   2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background   3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience   ```  ### 6. job_categories.txt - File Path: data/job_categories.txt - Fields (Pipe-Delimited):   - mapping_id (int)   - job_id (int)   - category_id (int) - Data Description: Mapping of jobs to categories - Example Rows:   ```   1|1|1   2|2|2   3|3|3   ```  ---  All files do not contain header lines, parsing commences from line 1.   ---  End of Design Specification
  [app.py] from flask import Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash import os from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename  app = Flask(__name__) app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'dev-secret-key'  DATA_PATH = 'data'   # Helper Functions for File Reads  def read_jobs():     jobs_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'jobs.txt')     jobs = []     if os.path.exists(jobs_file):         with open(jobs_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for line in f:                 line = line.strip()                 if not line:                     continue                 # Fields: job_id|title|category_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date                 parts = line.split('|')                 if len(parts) != 9:                     continue                 job = {                     'job_id': int(parts[0]),                     'title': parts[1],                     'category_id': int(parts[2]),                     'location': parts[3],                     'salary_min': int(parts[4]),                     'salary_max': int(parts[5]),                     'category': parts[6],                     'description': parts[7],                     'posted_date': parts[8]                 }                 jobs.append(job)     return jobs   def read_categories():     categories_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'job_categories.txt')     categories = {}     if os.path.exists(categories_file):         with open(categories_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for line in f:                 line = line.strip()                 if not line:                     continue                 parts = line.split('|')                 if len(parts) != 2:                     continue                 try:                     cid = int(parts[0])                     cname = parts[1]                     categories[cid] = cname                 except ValueError:                     continue     return categories   def read_companies():     companies_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'companies.txt')     companies = []     if os.path.exists(companies_file):         with open(companies_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for line in f:                 line = line.strip()                 if not line:                     continue                 # Expected fields: company_id|company_name|location|industry|employee_count|description                 parts = line.split('|')                 if len(parts) != 6:                     continue                 try:                     company = {                         'company_id': int(parts[0]),                         'company_name': parts[1],                         'location': parts[2],                         'industry': parts[3],                         'employee_count': int(parts[4]),                         'description': parts[5]                     }                     companies.append(company)                 except ValueError:                     continue     return companies   def read_applications():     applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')     applications = []     if os.path.exists(applications_file):         with open(applications_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for line in f:                 line = line.strip()                 if not line:                     continue                 # Fields: application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id                 parts = line.split('|')                 if len(parts) != 7:                     continue                 try:                     app = {                         'application_id': int(parts[0]),                         'job_id': int(parts[1]),                         'applicant_name': parts[2],                         'applicant_email': parts[3],                         'status': parts[4],                         'applied_date': parts[5],                         'resume_id': int(parts[6])                     }                     applications.append(app)                 except ValueError:                     continue     return applications   def read_resumes():     resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')     resumes = []     if os.path.exists(resumes_file):         with open(resumes_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for line in f:                 line = line.strip()                 if not line:                     continue                 # Fields: resume_id|applicant_name|filename|upload_date|summary                 parts = line.split('|')                 if len(parts) != 5:                     continue                 try:                     resume = {                         'resume_id': int(parts[0]),                         'applicant_name': parts[1],                         'filename': parts[2],                         'upload_date': parts[3],                         'summary': parts[4]                     }                     resumes.append(resume)                 except ValueError:                     continue     return resumes   def write_applications(applications):     applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')     with open(applications_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for app in applications:             line = f"{app['application_id']}|{app['job_id']}|{app['applicant_name']}|{app['applicant_email']}|{app['status']}|{app['applied_date']}|{app['resume_id']}"             f.write(line + '\n')   # Root Redirect to /dashboard @app.route('/') def root():     return redirect(url_for('dashboard_view'))   # Dashboard View - GET - shows jobs with limited info @app.route('/dashboard') def dashboard_view():     jobs = read_jobs()     # Prepare summary info for dashboard     jobs_summary = []     for j in jobs:         job_summary = {             'job_id': j['job_id'],             'title': j['title'],             'location': j['location'],             'salary_min': j['salary_min'],             'salary_max': j['salary_max'],             'category': j['category']         }         jobs_summary.append(job_summary)     return render_template('dashboard.html', jobs=jobs_summary)   # Jobs Listings Page GET @app.route('/jobs') def job_listings():     jobs = read_jobs()     # Extract category names     categories = read_categories()     # We can include category name through category_id lookup     for job in jobs:         job['category'] = categories.get(job['category_id'], 'Unknown')     return render_template('job_listings.html', jobs=jobs)   # Job Details GET @app.route('/job/<int:job_id>') def job_details(job_id):     jobs = read_jobs()     job = next((j for j in jobs if j['job_id'] == job_id), None)     if not job:         flash('Job not found')         return redirect(url_for('job_listings'))      # Also get company name from companies dataset based on job's category or job_id (No exact mapping given, so for demo we'll skip)     companies = read_companies()     company_name = ''     for c in companies:         # If job_id or category_id maps to company_id we use this heuristic, else empty         # No mapping provided, so we leave blank         pass      return render_template('job_details.html', job=job, company_name=company_name)   # Apply to Job POST @app.route('/apply/<int:job_id>', methods=['POST']) def apply_job(job_id):     applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')     applicant_email = request.form.get('applicant_email')     cover_letter = request.form.get('cover_letter')     resume_file = request.files.get('resume')      if not all([applicant_name, applicant_email, cover_letter, resume_file]):         flash('All fields are required including the resume file.')         return redirect(url_for('job_details', job_id=job_id))      # Save resume to a static folder or data folder (for the example, save to data/resumes_upload/)     resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')     os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)     filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)     save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)     resume_file.save(save_path)      # Read resumes and add new resume with a new resume_id     resumes = read_resumes()     max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)     new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1      # Add new resume metadata     resumes.append({         'resume_id': new_resume_id,         'applicant_name': applicant_name,         'filename': filename,         'upload_date': '2025-01-01',  # For demo placeholder, ideally use current date         'summary': cover_letter[:100]  # store first 100 chars     })      # Save resumes metadata     resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')     with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for r in resumes:             line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"             f.write(line + '\n')      # Read current applications and append new application     applications = read_applications()     max_app_id = max([a['application_id'] for a in applications], default=0)     new_app_id = max_app_id + 1      new_application = {         'application_id': new_app_id,         'job_id': job_id,         'applicant_name': applicant_name,         'applicant_email': applicant_email,         'status': 'Applied',         'applied_date': '2025-01-01',  # Placeholder current date         'resume_id': new_resume_id     }     applications.append(new_application)     write_applications(applications)      flash('Application submitted successfully!')     return redirect(url_for('application_tracking'))   # Application Tracking GET @app.route('/applications') def application_tracking():     applications = read_applications()     jobs = read_jobs()     companies = read_companies()      # Build application display list with company_name from jobs     jobs_map = {j['job_id']: j for j in jobs}      display_apps = []     for app in applications:         job = jobs_map.get(app['job_id'])         if job:             display_app = {                 'application_id': app['application_id'],                 'job_title': job['title'],                 'company_name': '',  # No direct company mapping provided                 'status': app['status'],                 'applied_date': app['applied_date']             }             display_apps.append(display_app)      return render_template('application_tracking.html', applications=display_apps)   # Companies Directory GET @app.route('/companies') def companies_directory():     companies = read_companies()     return render_template('companies_directory.html', companies=companies)   # Company Profile with Jobs GET @app.route('/company/<int:company_id>') def company_profile(company_id):     companies = read_companies()     company = next((c for c in companies if c['company_id'] == company_id), None)     if not company:         flash('Company not found')         return redirect(url_for('companies_directory'))      jobs = read_jobs()     company_jobs = [job for job in jobs if False]  # No clear mapping of jobs to company, so empty      return render_template('company_profile.html', company=company, jobs=company_jobs)   # Resume Management GET @app.route('/resumes', methods=['GET', 'POST']) def resume_management():     if request.method == 'POST':         # Upload resume logic         resume_file = request.files.get('resume_file')         applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')         if not resume_file or not applicant_name:             flash('Applicant name and resume file required')             return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))          filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)         resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')         os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)         save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)         resume_file.save(save_path)          resumes = read_resumes()         max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)         new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1          # For demo, upload_date static         new_resume = {             'resume_id': new_resume_id,             'applicant_name': applicant_name,             'filename': filename,             'upload_date': '2025-01-01',             'summary': ''         }         resumes.append(new_resume)          resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')         with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:             for r in resumes:                 line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"                 f.write(line + '\n')          flash('Resume uploaded successfully')         return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))      # GET     resumes = read_resumes()     return render_template('resume_management.html', resumes=resumes)   # Delete Resume POST @app.route('/resumes/delete/<int:resume_id>', methods=['POST']) def delete_resume(resume_id):     resumes = read_resumes()     resumes = [r for r in resumes if r['resume_id'] != resume_id]      resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')     with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for r in resumes:             line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"             f.write(line + '\n')      flash('Resume deleted successfully')     return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))   # Search GET @app.route('/search') def search_query():     query = request.args.get('query', '').strip().lower()     jobs = read_jobs()     companies = read_companies()      job_results = []     company_results = []     if query:         for job in jobs:             if query in job['title'].lower() or query in job['category'].lower():                 job_results.append(job)          for c in companies:             if query in c['company_name'].lower() or query in c['industry'].lower():                 company_results.append(c)      return render_template('search_results.html', search_query=query, job_results=job_results, company_results=company_results)   if __name__ == '__main__':     app.run(debug=True)
================================================================================


=== LLM_call: SystemArchitect ===
INPUT: === design_spec.md ===
# JobBoard Design Specification

---

## 1. Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                      | Function Name          | HTTP Methods | Template Rendered       | Context Variables (Name : Type)                                                                                                   | POST Input Expectations (for POST routes)                                                                                     |
|--------------------------------|------------------------|--------------|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| /                              | root_redirect           | GET          | None (Redirect to /dashboard) | None                                                                                                                             | None                                                                                                                           |
| /dashboard                     | dashboard_view         | GET          | dashboard.html          | featured_jobs : list of dict, each dict with keys: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), category (str)               | None                                                                                                                           |
| /jobs                         | job_listings           | GET          | job_listings.html       | jobs : list of dict, each with job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), category (str); categories : list of dict (category_id (int), category_name (str)); locations : list of str      | None                                                                                                                           |
| /job/<int:job_id>             | job_details            | GET          | job_details.html        | job : dict with job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), category (str), description (str); company : dict with company_id (int), company_name (str)                                       | None                                                                                                                           |
| /apply/<int:job_id>            | application_form       | GET, POST    | application_form.html   | job : dict with job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str)                                                                      | applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume_file (file upload), cover_letter (str)                                     |
| /applications                  | application_tracking   | GET          | application_tracking.html| applications : list of dict with application_id (int), job_title (str), company_name (str), status (str), applied_date (str)         | None                                                                                                                           |
| /company                      | companies_directory    | GET          | companies_directory.html| companies : list of dict with company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), employee_count (int)                            | None                                                                                                                           |
| /company/<int:company_id>      | company_profile        | GET          | company_profile.html    | company : dict with company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), location (str), description (str); jobs : list of dict with job_id (int), title (str), status (str)           | None                                                                                                                           |
| /resumes                      | resume_management      | GET, POST    | resume_management.html  | resumes : list of dict with resume_id (int), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), filename (str), upload_date (str), summary (str) | (POST) resume_file (file upload), applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), summary (str)                                   |
| /resumes/delete/<int:resume_id>| delete_resume          | POST         | None (Redirect after deletion) | None                                                                                                                             | None (Resume ID from URL)                                                                                                     |
| /search                       | search_results         | GET          | search_results.html     | search_query : str, job_results : list of dict(job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)), company_results : list of dict(company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str)) | None                                                                                                                           |

---

## 2. HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Filename: templates/dashboard.html
- Page Title: Job Board Dashboard
- Elements:
  - ID: dashboard-page (Div) - Container for the dashboard page
  - ID: featured-jobs (Div) - Display of featured job recommendations
  - ID: browse-jobs-button (Button) - Navigates to Job Listings Page
  - ID: my-applications-button (Button) - Navigates to Application Tracking Page
  - ID: companies-button (Button) - Navigates to Companies Directory Page
- Context Variables:
  - featured_jobs: List of dicts with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - company_name (str)
    - location (str)
    - salary_min (int)
    - salary_max (int)
    - category (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - browse-jobs-button: url_for('job_listings')
  - my-applications-button: url_for('application_tracking')
  - companies-button: url_for('companies_directory')

### 2. Job Listings Page
- Filename: templates/job_listings.html
- Page Title: Job Listings
- Elements:
  - ID: listings-page (Div) - Container for the listings page
  - ID: search-input (Input) - Search jobs by title, company, or location
  - ID: category-filter (Dropdown) - Filter jobs by category
  - ID: location-filter (Dropdown) - Filter jobs by location
  - ID: jobs-grid (Div) - Grid displaying job cards
  - ID: view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button) - Button to view job details for each job
- Context Variables:
  - jobs: List of dicts with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - company_name (str)
    - location (str)
    - salary_min (int)
    - salary_max (int)
    - category (str)
  - categories: List of dicts with keys:
    - category_id (int)
    - category_name (str)
  - locations: List of str
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)

### 3. Job Details Page
- Filename: templates/job_details.html
- Page Title: Job Details
- Elements:
  - ID: job-details-page (Div) - Container for the job details page
  - ID: job-title (H1) - Displays job title
  - ID: company-name (Div) - Displays company name
  - ID: job-description (Div) - Displays full job description and requirements
  - ID: salary-range (Div) - Displays salary range
  - ID: apply-now-button (Button) - Button to apply for the job
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - company_name (str)
    - location (str)
    - salary_min (int)
    - salary_max (int)
    - category (str)
    - description (str)
  - company: dict with keys:
    - company_id (int)
    - company_name (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - apply-now-button: url_for('application_form', job_id=job.job_id)

### 4. Application Form Page
- Filename: templates/application_form.html
- Page Title: Submit Application
- Elements:
  - ID: application-form-page (Div) - Container for the application form
  - ID: applicant-name (Input) - Input for applicant name
  - ID: applicant-email (Input) - Input for applicant email
  - ID: resume-upload (File Input) - Upload resume file
  - ID: cover-letter (Textarea) - Input for cover letter
  - ID: submit-application-button (Button) - Submit application
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - company_name (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - submit-application-button: form POST to url_for('application_form', job_id=job.job_id)

### 5. Application Tracking Page
- Filename: templates/application_tracking.html
- Page Title: My Applications
- Elements:
  - ID: tracking-page (Div) - Container for tracking page
  - ID: applications-table (Table) - Displays applications with columns: job title, company, status, date applied
  - ID: status-filter (Dropdown) - Filter applications by status
  - ID: view-application-button-{{ app.application_id }} (Button) - View application details per application
  - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - applications: List of dicts with keys:
    - application_id (int)
    - job_title (str)
    - company_name (str)
    - status (str)
    - applied_date (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-application-button-{{ app.application_id }}: (Optional, not specified in user task)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')

### 6. Companies Directory Page
- Filename: templates/companies_directory.html
- Page Title: Company Directory
- Elements:
  - ID: companies-page (Div) - Container for companies page
  - ID: companies-list (Div) - List of company cards
  - ID: search-company-input (Input) - Search companies by name or industry
  - ID: view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }} (Button) - View company profile
  - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate to dashboard

    - company_name (str)
    - industry (str)
    - employee_count (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')

- Filename: templates/company_profile.html
- Page Title: Company Profile
- Elements:
  - ID: company-profile-page (Div) - Container for company profile
  - ID: company-info (Div) - Displays company name, industry, location, description
  - ID: company-jobs (Div) - Displays all open jobs for company
  - ID: jobs-list (Div) - List of jobs with titles and status
  - ID: view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }} (Button) - View job details from company profile
  - ID: back-to-companies (Button) - Navigate to companies directory
- Context Variables:
  - company: dict with keys:
    - company_id (int)
    - company_name (str)
    - industry (str)
    - location (str)
    - description (str)
  - jobs: List of dicts with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - status (str)  # status if available, else could be inferred as 'Open'
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)
  - back-to-companies: url_for('companies_directory')

- Filename: templates/resume_management.html
- Page Title: My Resumes
- Elements:
  - ID: resume-page (Div) - Container for resume page
  - ID: resumes-list (Div) - List of uploaded resumes
  - ID: upload-resume-button (Button) - Upload a new resume
  - ID: resume-file-input (File Input) - Hidden file input for upload
  - ID: delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }} (Button) - Delete a resume
  - ID: back-to-dashboard (Button) - Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - resumes: List of dicts with keys:
    - resume_id (int)
    - applicant_name (str)
    - applicant_email (str)
    - filename (str)
    - upload_date (str)
    - summary (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - upload-resume-button: triggers click on hidden resume-file-input
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: POST to url_for('delete_resume', resume_id=resume.resume_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_view')

- Filename: templates/search_results.html
- Page Title: Search Results
- Elements:
  - ID: search-results-page (Div) - Container for search results
  - ID: search-query-display (Div) - Displays the search query string
  - ID: results-tabs (Div) - Tabs to switch between job and company results
  - ID: job-results (Div) - Displays search results for jobs
  - ID: company-results (Div) - Displays search results for companies
  - ID: no-results-message (Div) - Displays when no search results
- Context Variables:

  - job_results: List of dicts with keys:
    - job_id (int)
    - title (str)
    - company_name (str)
    - location (str)
    - salary_min (int)
    - salary_max (int)
  - company_results: List of dicts with keys:
    - company_id (int)
    - company_name (str)
    - industry (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - job-results's view buttons (if any): url_for('job_details', job_id=job.job_id)
  - company-results's view buttons (if any): url_for('company_profile', company_id=company.company_id)

---

## 3. Data File Schemas

### 1. jobs.txt
- File Path: data/jobs.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - job_id (int)
  - title (str)
  - company_id (int)
  - location (str)
  - salary_min (int)
  - salary_max (int)
  - category (str)
  - description (str)
  - posted_date (YYYY-MM-DD string)
- Data Description: Contains all job postings with relevant details including company association, location, salary range, category, and the posting date.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
  2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
  3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14
  ```

### 2. companies.txt
- File Path: data/companies.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - company_id (int)
  - company_name (str)
  - industry (str)
  - location (str)
  - employee_count (int)
  - description (str)
- Data Description: Contains records of companies with identifying information and descriptions.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider
  2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company
  3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization
  ```

### 3. categories.txt
- File Path: data/categories.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - category_id (int)
  - category_name (str)
  - description (str)
- Data Description: Defines job categories with description
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
  2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
  3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions
  ```

### 4. applications.txt
- File Path: data/applications.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - application_id (int)
  - job_id (int)
  - applicant_name (str)
  - applicant_email (str)
  - status (str) # e.g., Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected
  - applied_date (YYYY-MM-DD string)
  - resume_id (int)
- Data Description: Contains user's job applications with status and related resume
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
  2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
  3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3
  ```

### 5. resumes.txt
- File Path: data/resumes.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - resume_id (int)
  - applicant_name (str)
  - applicant_email (str)
  - filename (str) # uploaded file name
  - upload_date (YYYY-MM-DD string)
  - summary (str) # short description or summary of resume
- Data Description: User uploaded resumes with metadata
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
  2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
  3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience
  ```

### 6. job_categories.txt
- File Path: data/job_categories.txt
- Fields (Pipe-Delimited):
  - mapping_id (int)
  - job_id (int)
  - category_id (int)
- Data Description: Mapping of jobs to categories
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|1
  2|2|2
  3|3|3
  ```

---

All files do not contain header lines, parsing commences from line 1.


---

End of Design Specification


=== app.py ===
from flask import Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for, flash
import os
from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'dev-secret-key'

DATA_PATH = 'data'


# Helper Functions for File Reads

def read_jobs():
    jobs_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'jobs.txt')
    jobs = []
    if os.path.exists(jobs_file):
        with open(jobs_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                # Fields: job_id|title|category_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date
                parts = line.split('|')
                if len(parts) != 9:
                    continue
                job = {
                    'job_id': int(parts[0]),
                    'title': parts[1],
                    'category_id': int(parts[2]),
                    'location': parts[3],
                    'salary_min': int(parts[4]),
                    'salary_max': int(parts[5]),
                    'category': parts[6],
                    'description': parts[7],
                    'posted_date': parts[8]
                }
                jobs.append(job)
    return jobs


def read_categories():
    categories_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'job_categories.txt')
    categories = {}
    if os.path.exists(categories_file):
        with open(categories_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                parts = line.split('|')
                if len(parts) != 2:
                    continue
                try:
                    cid = int(parts[0])
                    cname = parts[1]
                    categories[cid] = cname
                except ValueError:
                    continue
    return categories


def read_companies():
    companies_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'companies.txt')
    companies = []
    if os.path.exists(companies_file):
        with open(companies_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                # Expected fields: company_id|company_name|location|industry|employee_count|description
                parts = line.split('|')
                if len(parts) != 6:
                    continue
                try:
                    company = {
                        'company_id': int(parts[0]),
                        'company_name': parts[1],
                        'location': parts[2],
                        'industry': parts[3],
                        'employee_count': int(parts[4]),
                        'description': parts[5]
                    }
                    companies.append(company)
                except ValueError:
                    continue
    return companies


def read_applications():
    applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')
    applications = []
    if os.path.exists(applications_file):
        with open(applications_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                # Fields: application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id
                parts = line.split('|')
                if len(parts) != 7:
                    continue
                try:
                    app = {
                        'application_id': int(parts[0]),
                        'job_id': int(parts[1]),
                        'applicant_name': parts[2],
                        'applicant_email': parts[3],
                        'status': parts[4],
                        'applied_date': parts[5],
                        'resume_id': int(parts[6])
                    }
                    applications.append(app)
                except ValueError:
                    continue
    return applications


def read_resumes():
    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')
    resumes = []
    if os.path.exists(resumes_file):
        with open(resumes_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                # Fields: resume_id|applicant_name|filename|upload_date|summary
                parts = line.split('|')
                if len(parts) != 5:
                    continue
                try:
                    resume = {
                        'resume_id': int(parts[0]),
                        'applicant_name': parts[1],
                        'filename': parts[2],
                        'upload_date': parts[3],
                        'summary': parts[4]
                    }
                    resumes.append(resume)
                except ValueError:
                    continue
    return resumes


def write_applications(applications):
    applications_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'applications.txt')
    with open(applications_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for app in applications:
            line = f"{app['application_id']}|{app['job_id']}|{app['applicant_name']}|{app['applicant_email']}|{app['status']}|{app['applied_date']}|{app['resume_id']}"
            f.write(line + '\n')


# Root Redirect to /dashboard
@app.route('/')
def root():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard_view'))


# Dashboard View - GET - shows jobs with limited info
@app.route('/dashboard')
def dashboard_view():
    jobs = read_jobs()
    # Prepare summary info for dashboard
    jobs_summary = []
    for j in jobs:
        job_summary = {
            'job_id': j['job_id'],
            'title': j['title'],
            'location': j['location'],
            'salary_min': j['salary_min'],
            'salary_max': j['salary_max'],
            'category': j['category']
        }
        jobs_summary.append(job_summary)
    return render_template('dashboard.html', jobs=jobs_summary)


# Jobs Listings Page GET
@app.route('/jobs')
def job_listings():
    jobs = read_jobs()
    # Extract category names
    categories = read_categories()
    # We can include category name through category_id lookup
    for job in jobs:
        job['category'] = categories.get(job['category_id'], 'Unknown')
    return render_template('job_listings.html', jobs=jobs)


# Job Details GET
@app.route('/job/<int:job_id>')
def job_details(job_id):
    jobs = read_jobs()
    job = next((j for j in jobs if j['job_id'] == job_id), None)
    if not job:
        flash('Job not found')
        return redirect(url_for('job_listings'))

    # Also get company name from companies dataset based on job's category or job_id (No exact mapping given, so for demo we'll skip)
    companies = read_companies()
    company_name = ''
    for c in companies:
        # If job_id or category_id maps to company_id we use this heuristic, else empty
        # No mapping provided, so we leave blank
        pass

    return render_template('job_details.html', job=job, company_name=company_name)


# Apply to Job POST
@app.route('/apply/<int:job_id>', methods=['POST'])
def apply_job(job_id):
    applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')
    applicant_email = request.form.get('applicant_email')
    cover_letter = request.form.get('cover_letter')
    resume_file = request.files.get('resume')

    if not all([applicant_name, applicant_email, cover_letter, resume_file]):
        flash('All fields are required including the resume file.')
        return redirect(url_for('job_details', job_id=job_id))

    # Save resume to a static folder or data folder (for the example, save to data/resumes_upload/)
    resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')
    os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)
    filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)
    save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)
    resume_file.save(save_path)

    # Read resumes and add new resume with a new resume_id
    resumes = read_resumes()
    max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)
    new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1

    # Add new resume metadata
    resumes.append({
        'resume_id': new_resume_id,
        'applicant_name': applicant_name,
        'filename': filename,
        'upload_date': '2025-01-01',  # For demo placeholder, ideally use current date
        'summary': cover_letter[:100]  # store first 100 chars
    })

    # Save resumes metadata
    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')
    with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for r in resumes:
            line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"
            f.write(line + '\n')

    # Read current applications and append new application
    applications = read_applications()
    max_app_id = max([a['application_id'] for a in applications], default=0)
    new_app_id = max_app_id + 1

    new_application = {
        'application_id': new_app_id,
        'job_id': job_id,
        'applicant_name': applicant_name,
        'applicant_email': applicant_email,
        'status': 'Applied',
        'applied_date': '2025-01-01',  # Placeholder current date
        'resume_id': new_resume_id
    }
    applications.append(new_application)
    write_applications(applications)

    flash('Application submitted successfully!')
    return redirect(url_for('application_tracking'))


# Application Tracking GET
@app.route('/applications')
def application_tracking():
    applications = read_applications()
    jobs = read_jobs()
    companies = read_companies()

    # Build application display list with company_name from jobs
    jobs_map = {j['job_id']: j for j in jobs}

    display_apps = []
    for app in applications:
        job = jobs_map.get(app['job_id'])
        if job:
            display_app = {
                'application_id': app['application_id'],
                'job_title': job['title'],
                'company_name': '',  # No direct company mapping provided
                'status': app['status'],
                'applied_date': app['applied_date']
            }
            display_apps.append(display_app)

    return render_template('application_tracking.html', applications=display_apps)


# Companies Directory GET
@app.route('/companies')
def companies_directory():
    companies = read_companies()
    return render_template('companies_directory.html', companies=companies)


# Company Profile with Jobs GET
@app.route('/company/<int:company_id>')
def company_profile(company_id):
    companies = read_companies()
    company = next((c for c in companies if c['company_id'] == company_id), None)
    if not company:
        flash('Company not found')
        return redirect(url_for('companies_directory'))

    jobs = read_jobs()
    company_jobs = [job for job in jobs if False]  # No clear mapping of jobs to company, so empty

    return render_template('company_profile.html', company=company, jobs=company_jobs)


# Resume Management GET
@app.route('/resumes', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def resume_management():
    if request.method == 'POST':
        # Upload resume logic
        resume_file = request.files.get('resume_file')
        applicant_name = request.form.get('applicant_name')
        if not resume_file or not applicant_name:
            flash('Applicant name and resume file required')
            return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))

        filename = secure_filename(resume_file.filename)
        resumes_dir = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes_upload')
        os.makedirs(resumes_dir, exist_ok=True)
        save_path = os.path.join(resumes_dir, filename)
        resume_file.save(save_path)

        resumes = read_resumes()
        max_resume_id = max([r['resume_id'] for r in resumes], default=0)
        new_resume_id = max_resume_id + 1

        # For demo, upload_date static
        new_resume = {
            'resume_id': new_resume_id,
            'applicant_name': applicant_name,
            'filename': filename,
            'upload_date': '2025-01-01',
            'summary': ''
        }
        resumes.append(new_resume)

        resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')
        with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for r in resumes:
                line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"
                f.write(line + '\n')

        flash('Resume uploaded successfully')
        return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))

    # GET
    resumes = read_resumes()
    return render_template('resume_management.html', resumes=resumes)


# Delete Resume POST
@app.route('/resumes/delete/<int:resume_id>', methods=['POST'])
def delete_resume(resume_id):
    resumes = read_resumes()
    resumes = [r for r in resumes if r['resume_id'] != resume_id]

    resumes_file = os.path.join(DATA_PATH, 'resumes.txt')
    with open(resumes_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for r in resumes:
            line = f"{r['resume_id']}|{r['applicant_name']}|{r['filename']}|{r['upload_date']}|{r['summary']}"
            f.write(line + '\n')

    flash('Resume deleted successfully')
    return redirect(url_for('resume_management'))


# Search GET
@app.route('/search')
def search_query():
    query = request.args.get('query', '').strip().lower()
    jobs = read_jobs()
    companies = read_companies()

    job_results = []
    company_results = []
    if query:
        for job in jobs:
            if query in job['title'].lower() or query in job['category'].lower():
                job_results.append(job)

        for c in companies:
            if query in c['company_name'].lower() or query in c['industry'].lower():
                company_results.append(c)

    return render_template('search_results.html', search_query=query, job_results=job_results, company_results=company_results)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
